name: Caño Cristales
description: An explosion of natural color known as "the river that ran away from paradise."

name: Las Lajas Sanctuary
description: A dangling church built over a Colombian gorge.

name: Tequendama Falls Museum
description: This picturesque mansion has gone from opulent ruin to nature museum.

name: Guatapé
description: The Andean town where every single building is decorated with a brightly colored frieze.

name: Ábaco Libros y Café
description: A cozy bookstore and coffee shop in the heart of Cartagena's Old City.

name: Tatacoa Desert
description: Unparalleled stargazing, one of Colombia's best observatories, and a swimming pool can all be found in the middle of this ancient tropical forest.

name: Cave of Splendor
description: Enchanting cave with a river crashing through the roof.

name: The Volcan de Lodo El Totumo
description: The caldera of this mini-volcano has been turned into a naturally heated mud bath.

name: Ciudad Perdida
description: An ancient city hidden in the heart of the Sierra Nevada mountains.

name: Nemocón Salt Mine
description: Colorful lights and mirror-like brine pools create trippy illusions within the historic mine.

name: El Peñon de Guatape
description: A 10 million-ton rock once worshiped by the Tahamies Indians, accessed by an astounding staircase.

name: La Puerta Falsa
description: This tiny shop has been serving traditional tamales for more than 200 years.

name: Cathedral of Salt
description: Cathedral carved out of salt, located a mile below the Earth's surface.

name: Jaime Duque Park
description: A little known Colombian theme park of giant curiosities.

name: The Gondolas of Medellín
description: An unusual and beautiful form of mass transit serves the city's steepest neighborhoods.

name: The Palace of the Inquisition
description: This elegant Colombian fortress was one of the torture headquarters for the Spanish Inquisition.

name: Bichacue Yath Arte & Naturaleza
description: A magical world of elves and fairies tucked amongst the trees in Colombia.

name: Wax Palms of Cocora Valley
description: The world's tallest palm trees look like the whimsical kind of plant you'd find in a Dr. Seuss story.

name: Hacienda Nápoles
description: Former drug lord’s manor, now a theme park. Watch out for the hippos.

name: Museo del Oro
description: This Bogotá museum houses the world's largest collection of pre-Columbian gold relics.

name: Convento de la Popa
description: Perched atop the highest hill in Cartagena, this 17th-century convent offers spectacular views of the city.

name: Casa Terracota
description: This whimsical home made entirely from clay is basically a giant piece of pottery.

name: Windows of Tisquizoque
description: A three-tiered waterfall plummets from a mountain cave with amazing views over the countryside.

name: Lake of Guatavita
description: Known as the site of 'El Dorado,' this mysterious lake is rumored to hold gold beneath its waters.

name: Parque de las Luces
description: Three hundred illuminated pillars form an “artificial forest” in Medellín.

name: La Mano del Gigante (The Giant’s Hand)
description: The enormous appendage offers spectacular views of Colombia's Huila Department.

name: Bogotá Botanical Garden
description: Colombia's biggest botanical garden contains plants from all across the country.

name: Comuna 13
description: Outdoor escalators and art have helped change what was once a notoriously dangerous part of Medellín.

name: Central Cemetery of Bogotá
description: Storied crypts fill the most famous cemetery in Colombia.

name: Casa Museo Pablo Escobar
description: This unofficial museum details the rise and fall of Colombia's most notorious narcotrafficker, Pablo Escobar.

name: The 'Wise Old Men' of El Gallineral Park
description: The centuries-old trees are draped with Spanish moss to make them look like wizened elders.

name: Plaza Botero
description: Home to 23 voluptuous and instantly lovable sculptures by one of Latin America's most celebrated artists.

name: Plaza Botero
description: Home to 23 voluptuous and instantly lovable sculptures by one of Latin America's most celebrated artists.

name: Door Knockers of Cartagena
description: The colonial city's social history is reflected in its fanciful door knockers.

name: San Pedro Claver Museum and Cloisters
description: An inspired museum dedicated to the patron saint of enslaved people, inside the cloisters where he lived and died.

name: Blue Anoles of Gorgona Island
description: The world's only pure blue lizard lives exclusively on this secluded tropical island.

name: Mummies of San Bernardo
description: No one knows for sure why this town's dead refuse to rot.

name: Tierradentro National Archaeological Park
description: This collection of ancient, mountaintop, underground tombs was once hidden by decades of bloodshed.

name: Darien Gap
description: Only gap in the 30,000-mile span of roads called the Pan-American Highway.

name: Sumapaz Páramo
description: The world's largest ecosystem of its kind is home to South America's only native species of bear.

name: La Casa de las Piedritas
description: This exceptional home was a 32-year labor of love by a man determined to build his wife the most beautiful stone house in the world.

name: Monserrate Sanctuary
description: Pilgrims each carried a brick to the top of Mount Monserrate to help build this historic sanctuary in Colombia.

name: Tatacoa Astronomia
description: A self-taught astronomer fell in love with the stars and built his own observatory in Colombia.

name: Museo Casa de la Memoria (House of Memory Museum)
description: A museum of memories in what was once one of the most violent cities in the world.

name: The Birds of Peace
description: Two identical statues by Fernando Botero, one blown to pieces in a terrorist bombing, the other donated as a gift for peace.

name: Museo El Fósil
description: A museum built around a bus-sized fossil on the side of the highway.

name: Museo Botero
description: A celebration of Colombia’s premier contemporary artist.

name: Train Track To Nowhere
description: A narrow ghost town built onto a peninsula behind a Colombian Slum.

name: Parque de los Pies Descalzos (Barefoot Park)
description: This city park wants you to take your shoes off.

name: Cat Park - Parque El Gato De Tejada
description: A park with one large cat sculpture and 15 smaller ones, all painted by different Colombian artists.

name: Andoke Foundation
description: A scalable map of Colombia with the bonus of a butterfly house.

name: National Observatory of Colombia
description: Erected in 1803, it is the first astronomical observatory that was built in the Americas.

name: Bioparque Los Ocarros (Los Ocarros Biopark)
description: A rehabilitation center for some of South America's most unique and endangered animals.

name: Guatavita la Nueva
description: The entire town looks like a slice of Spain that was carted across the ocean and plunked in the Andes.

name: The Chicherias of Bogota's La Candelaria Barrio
description: This narrow street is packed with street vendors and bars selling chicha.

name: Villa de Leyva
description: This colonial town in Colombia sits around the largest stone paved square in South America.

name: Parque Arvi
description: Located outside Medellín, this Colombian park focuses on conservation and sustainability.

name: Virgen Rosa Mística (Virgin of the Mystic Rose)
description: The religious shrine that Pablo Escobar's men would allegedly visit before completing a mission.

name: Gorgona Island Prison Ruins
description: This maximum-security prison was abandoned to make way for a national park.

name: Pablo Escobar's Grave
description: The (usually) serene burial place of the world's most notorious drug lord.

name: Teleférico la Garrucha (Garrucha Cable Car)
description: Would you dare to dangle from a great height in a wooden basket?

name: Puente de Occidente (Bridge of the West)
description: What was once the longest suspension bridge in Latin America was directly inspired by the iconic Brooklyn Bridge.

name: BD Bacatá
description: The world's first crowdfunded skyscraper has not gone quite to plan.

name: Shakira Monument
description: This 16-foot, 6-ton statue in the singer's hometown doesn't actually look like her.

name: La Alpujarra Administrative Center
description: In the heart of Medellín, an artsy urban complex sets the stage for demonstrations and protests.

name: Parque Santander
description: This small city park is best known for the nightly convergence of thousands of shrieking parakeets.

name: La Ciclovía de Bogotá
description: Every Sunday, Bogotá turns 75 miles of its city streets into a bicycle superhighway.

name: La Siberia
description: An abandoned company town and cement factory outside of Bogotá, now haunted by ghosts and scofflaws.

name: Diego Felipe Becerra's Bridge
description: At the site of a graffiti artist's murder, fellow artists gather to pay homage.

name: Museo MAPUKA
description: Explore the rich history of Colombia's Indigenous peoples.

name: Jurubirá Hot Springs
description: Natural hot springs hidden in the rainforest are only accessible by boat.

name: Sugarcane Museum - Hacienda Piedechinche Museo de la Caña de Azucar
description: A museum showcasing the history of sugarcane with a rich history.

name: Light Kiosk
description: Colombia's first concrete structure doubles as a replica of Versaille's Petit Trianon Belvedere and a symbol for development.

name: Fundación Natütama
description: From pink dolphins to underwater cities, this museum explores the unseen, mythical world of the Amazon river.

name: Cañon del Guejar (Güejar River Canyon)
description: River raft through rural Colombia’s ancient rock formations, pristine landscapes, and towering waterfalls.

name: Quinta de San Pedro Alejandrino
description: The 17th-century hacienda where Simón Bolívar spent his last days before dying from tuberculosis.

name: Museo Geológico Nacional José Royo y Gómez
description: A small museum filled with unique fossil finds, including one of the oldest specimens of ground sloths found in Colombia.

name: Batería del Ángel San Rafael
description: An 18th-century battery complete with original kitchen and garrison.

name: Vargas Swamp Lancers Memorial
description: Largest monument in Colombia depicts 14 lancers who drove back an army.

name: Bolívar Desnudo (Nude Bolívar)
description: This unusual monument shows a naked Simón Bolívar stripped of clothing, medals, and swords.

name: Parque del Perro
description: This statue pays homage to a group of kids's childhood companion who once called the park home.

name: Armero, Colombia
description: A town half-submerged in volcanic mud has become a pilgrimage site for devotees of one of the tragedy's victims.

name: Punta Gallinas Lighthouse
description: A lighthouse marking the northernmost point of continental South America.

name: Casa del Marqués de San Jorge
description: This archaeological museum in an 18th-century mansion showcases the country’s finest examples of pre-Columbian ceramics.

name: La Gaitana Monument
description: This dramatic monument in Colombia honors a little-known indigenous fighter for independence.

name: Column of the Freedom of the Slaves
description: Colombia’s only monument erected to mark the end of slavery in the former Republic of New Granada.

name: Salto de Guadalupe
description: Ride a steep cable car alongside a tall waterfall that feeds a near century-old power plant.

name: Puente de Boyacá
description: This small bridge serves as a symbol of South America's independence.

name: Hoyo Soplador (Blowhole)
description: This sea-based blowhole releases seawater when waves hit the coastal caves.

name: The Largest Coffee Cup in Colombia
description: Celebrating Colombian coffee growers with a big gulp of a sculpture.

name: La Linterna
description: Not just another old printing press; it's a place of cultural resistance and a history of the city.

name: El Pozo de Hunzahúa (Hunzahúa Well)
description: This well in the former capital of Colombia's Muisca people is the setting for a steamy legend.

name: Pablo Escobar's Abandoned Secret Island Mansion
description: Hidden amid the tropical forest in the Caribbean Sea are the decaying ruins of the Cocaine King's luxurious island party home.

name: KGB Cartagena
description: In the middle of one of South America's most historic walled cities lies a bar with an enormous collection of Soviet memorabilia.

name: La Manuela Hacienda Ruins
description: Ripped apart by a bomb in 1993, the shell of Pablo Escobar's infamous vacation house still stands.

name: The Circo Teatro of Old Cartagena
description: A mouldering Colombian bull ring stands as a reminder of the country's complicated relationship with a brutal sport.
